about

Celebrating the 50th anniversary of The Beatles' classic.

The Beatles’ ABBEY ROAD album was released on 26th September 1969.
Mike Westbrook’s OFF ABBEY ROAD was commissioned by Commune di Reggio Emilia, Italy and premiered in December 1988 as part of a Festival celebrating the music of The Beatles. OFF ABBEY ROAD was toured internationally by the Mike Westbrook Band and recorded for Enja Records at the Willisau Jazz Festival in August 1989. This later version, AFTER ABBEY ROAD, was recorded at the Gostenhofer Jazztage, Nuremberg on 5th October 1996. She Loves You was recorded at the Willisau Jazz Festival on 27th August 2009 (with Richard Newby on drums).

Available as a digital download only.

credits

released September 27, 2019

Personnel
John Winfield voice
Kate Westbrook voice, tenor horn, piccolo
Brian Godding guitar
Pete Whyman alto saxophone and clarinet
Alan Wakeman baritone and soprano saxophones
Mike Westbrook piano
Andy Grappy tuba
Peter Fairclough drums
on “She Loves You” Richard Newby replaces
Peter Fairclough on drums.

Recorded live in Nuremberg 1996

Edited by Matthew North
Re-mastered by John Winfield
Design by Sam Whitney-Morris
Photography by Kevin McDonnell
with thanks to The Beatles and George Martin for creating ABBEY ROAD

about

Mike Westbrook London, UK

Mike Westbrook, composer and pianist, has received many honours and his outpouring of work in a career stretching over 50 years, ranges from scores for full orchestra to solo piano improvisation.

  1. westbrookjazz.co.uk
  2. Facebook
  3. Kate Westbrook

    supported by 12 fans who also own “After Abbey Road”

    remember hearing Tanglwood '63 on car radio (BBC Jazz in Britain likely) whilst travelling home on A55 in North Wales late at night in 1971; stunning! This version slightly less rehearsed but fascinating nontheless; essential listening for Jazz Rock fans peelthepaint
